mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market and consumer demand, which typically supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a medium horizon. This is an always-on trend signal reflecting economic momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of employment with equity markets
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window, or the correlation turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is exceptionally low, it signals an accommodative monetary policy environment, reducing the cost of capital for businesses and making equities more attractive, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:central bank policy impact on asset prices
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% for at least three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer health and spending power. This directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns as the market prices in improved economic fundamentals.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3mo avg growth > 0.5% AND UNRATE 3mo avg change < -0.1%
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and UNRATE change shows no statistically significant positive predictive power for SPX 20-day returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, increasing industrial production (INDPRO) signals a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This boosts corporate profits and drives the S&P 500 higher, with the effect being particularly strong when emerging from a recessionary trough.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial activity as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery periods, INDPRO growth does not significantly predict positive SPX 60-day returns, or its predictive power is no different from other economic regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings. This typically results in negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate an economic downturn.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong underlying demand and investment in the real estate sector. This has positive spillover effects on related industries and overall economic sentiment, supporting equity markets over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market as a bellwether for consumer confidence
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with SPX 5-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and stable, combined with an accommodative federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), it creates a 'goldilocks' scenario for equities. Low discount rates and stable purchasing power drive positive S&P 500 returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low inflation and rates support equity valuations
failure_condition: In periods meeting the combined trigger conditions, SPX 60-day returns are not significantly positive, or the interaction term fails to show robust predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic slowdown or contraction, a significant decline in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates weakening corporate earnings and consumer confidence. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in a recession and reduced future profitability.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP contractions correlate with bear markets
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where GDPC1 shows negative quarter-over-quarter annualized growth.
